element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn auto-grapple on contact; combinations of joypad and buttons unlock holds and slams
Button-mashing exercises (squats, situps, push-ups) fill progress meter to increase move damage; seven sphere progression gates total power
Ground attacks, top-rope aerial attacks, running attacks introduced contextually during matches

element_introduction_order.first_appears: First signature move on weakened opponent
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Game interrupts with TV-style close-up replays of finishers; rewards player understanding of move timing
Three-count pinfall, submission, and count-out (20-second ring-out timer); mutual count-out counts as loss for player
Meter rapidly swings between players; determines grapple advantage; adds symmetrical competitive tension
10 playable wrestlers with different heights, weights, and signature moves; specialties encourage move discovery
Non-playable 'Blue King' (Earl of Doom) serves as climax fight with undocumented move set

typical_relief_triggers: Performing action replay on weakened opponent
typical_tension_triggers: Loss of training sphere after match defeat
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based cycle with inter-match training cooldown; tension escalates through undefeated tournament run; momentum meter creates real-time oscillation in 2P mode

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial roster opponents with balanced stats; first sphere (power level) sufficient for early wins
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Training becomes mandatory; loss of spheres on defeat creates progression gate; opponent strength increases with roster advancement
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final boss Blue King presents skill wall; undocumented moveset requires mastery of all learned mechanics
notable_spikes: First match loss (sphere penalty and retraining required)
Blue King final boss encounter (non-playable, unique moveset)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player mode chains matches sequentially against all 10 playable wrestlers, culminating in final boss. Victory in each match unlocks next opponent; defeat costs training sphere and resets progression.

Between each match, player enters training mode with three button-mashing options (squats, situps, push-ups) to fill progress meter and add training spheres. Maximum 7 spheres; loss during match reduces count.
Signature moves executed on weakened opponents trigger cinematic replay, interrupting real-time gameplay with close-up television-style moment. First game feature of this type.
